# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:18-slim

# Create non-root user for running the application
RUN groupadd -r nodeapp && useradd -r -g nodeapp -s /bin/false nodeapp

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Update package lists and install security updates
RUN apt-get update && apt-get upgrade -y

# Install build tools
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential

# Install Python (often needed for node-gyp)
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3

# Install git
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git

# Install curl
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl

# Install wget
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends wget

# Install ca-certificates for secure HTTPS connections
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates

# Clean up apt cache to reduce image size
RUN apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy package files with appropriate ownership
COPY --chown=nodeapp:nodeapp package*.json ./

# Install Node.js dependencies
RUN npm ci --only=production && npm cache clean --force

# Copy application code with appropriate ownership
COPY --chown=nodeapp:nodeapp . .

# Remove write permissions from application files
RUN chmod -R 555 /app

# Create directory for application data with proper permissions
RUN mkdir -p /app/data && chown nodeapp:nodeapp /app/data && chmod 700 /app/data

# Switch to non-root user
USER nodeapp

# Expose application port (adjust as needed)
EXPOSE 3000

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD node -e "require('http').get('http://localhost:3000/health', (r) => {process.exit(r.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1)})"

# Start application
CMD ["node", "index.js"]
